Home/International Removals/the Netherlands
The Move

The Netherlands is one of our quickest European routes, and one of our busiest.

From Amsterdam to Rotterdam, British professionals and families relocate to the Netherlands regularly, and the short crossing keeps things fast. Dedicated vehicles run on a tight schedule, so your belongings rarely wait long.

Road freight keeps a Dutch move quick and flexible. A dedicated load gives you the fastest door to door service, while a part load shares vehicle space and splits the cost.

Popular destinations we deliver to
AmsterdamRotterdamThe HagueUtrechtEindhovenMaastricht
Typical Transit Times
Dedicated road service2–4 days
Part-load (groupage)1 week
Times shown are door to door. Please allow a little extra for customs formalities. We recommend booking 4 to 6 weeks ahead of your move date.
Your options

Choose the right way to move

Every move to the Netherlands is different, so we shape the service around yours.

Road · Dedicated

A vehicle reserved for your move

One crew loads at your door in the UK and the same crew delivers a few days later. Nothing is transhipped and nothing waits in a depot.

Best for: full households, fixed dates
Road · Part-load

Share the vehicle, share the cost

Your goods travel alongside other moves on a scheduled departure. A little more patience, a noticeably smaller bill.

Best for: smaller moves, flexible budgets
Fast Crossing

One of our shortest European routes

The short Channel crossing means Dutch deliveries are often among the quickest moves we run.

Best for: movers wanting speed
Excess Baggage

A few boxes or suitcases

Not moving a whole household? Our excess baggage service is a simple, affordable way to send personal items and smaller consignments overseas.

Best for: light movers & students
Good to know

Moving to the Netherlands now takes paperwork, and we handle it

UK households entering the Netherlands pass through customs, and the right documents make all the difference. We prepare everything with you well before moving day, so your belongings keep moving.

Customs declarations

Your shipment travels with a full inventory and the declarations Dutch customs expect.

BSN registration

Your BSN, the Dutch citizen service number, often supports your duty relief application.

Duty relief on household goods

Used personal effects usually enter free of duty when you transfer your main residence to the Netherlands.

Residency evidence

Proof of your move, such as a visa or registration, supports the relief claim.
